resources.string: This application is for educational and preliminary design purposes only. All calculations should be verified by a licensed structural engineer. The developer is not responsible for any errors or damages resulting from the use of this application.
Copyright \u00a9 2024–2025 Eng. Ahmed Magdy. All rights reserved.

Civil Engineer Pro is developed by Eng. Ahmed Magdy, a structural engineer specializing in concrete and steel design.

Contact:
Email: eng.ahmedmagdy121314@gmail.com
Phone: +20 101 262 8353
This device appears to be rooted. For security reasons, some features may be limited.
App integrity check failed. Please reinstall from the official source.

DISCLAIMER: This application is a professional tool for educational and preliminary design purposes only. All structural calculations, designs, and outputs must be verified by a licensed structural engineer before any construction use. The developer (Eng. Ahmed Magdy) assumes no liability for errors, damages, or any consequences resulting from the use of this application. Use at your own risk.
Start your next structural project with precision and professionalism\nSupports Egyptian Code ECP 203, American ACI 318, and Saudi SBC 304
Start your first design and it will appear here
Developed by CivilEG Team — Specialized Software Engineering
Notice: This application is for educational and preliminary design purposes only. All calculations should be verified by a licensed structural engineer.
Developed by CivilEG Team — Specialized Software Engineering
No projects available. A default project will be created.
Add nodes or use a template
Node %d: dx=%s mm, dy=%s mm, θ=%s rad
Node %d: Rx=%s kN, Ry=%s kN, M=%s kN.m
Weight: %s kg/m | Ix=%s cm⁴ | Sx=%s cm³
💡 Dead Load Only: Used in temporary structures or special conditions with no live loads.
💡 Dead + Live: Most common and safe design combination, includes structure weight, furniture, and occupants.
Strong Axis Moment of Inertia Iy
Weak Axis Moment of Inertia Iz
Strong Axis Radius of Gyration ry
Weak Axis Radius of Gyration rz
What would you like to calculate today?
Add your first project to get started
HI & RL Calculations for Survey Points
HI = RL (BM) + Backsight Reading\nRL (Point) = HI - Foresight Reading
Press + to create a new project
Save Retaining Wall Design in Project
Inventory is empty. Add new items.
